title: Blood on Denim
timeEstimate: 10–30 minutes; cold-only protein, indigo + ring-risk warnings
stainChemistry: Blood is protein with iron in it. Fresh blood is water-soluble in cold conditions and lifts easily. Heat or time turns the iron from red to brown and locks the protein into the fabric — once that happens, the stain is much harder. On denim, two complications stack: indigo dye sits on the surface of the fiber and any oxidizer (chlorine bleach, concentrated peroxide) strips color along with the stain, and aggressive scrubbing on dark denim leaves a halo ring. The recurring trap on blood is reaching for hot water — exactly what locks it. The other trap is reaching for chlorine bleach on dark denim — strips indigo unevenly.
whyThisWorks: Cold water keeps the protein from setting and rinses out the soluble portion fast. An enzyme pretreat (cool water only — heat damages the enzymes AND the protein) breaks down the residual protein. 3% peroxide on a damp cloth handles any iron-brown shadow on light-wash denim where a hidden test passes — bounded application is key. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY locks nothing.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): blood is hemoglobin + plasma proteins + iron. Hemoglobin oxidizes from Fe²⁺ (red) to Fe³⁺ (brown) over hours. Pro uses cold liquid Protein Spotter (protease pretreat) plus NSD plus sodium hydrosulfite at controlled dilution for set/oxidized blood — reducing bleach is denim-safer than chlorine bleach because it doesn't strip indigo as aggressively.
defaultAssumption: Cut, kid scrape, period stain, or kitchen mishap on jeans, fresh and not yet hot-washed. Hot-washed or hot-dried = often permanent brown shadow. Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim = pro from minute one (any moisture causes uneven fading).

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold/ice water, white catch towel, gloves
spottingProtocol.instruction: Gloves on (biohazard, especially if not your blood). Hold the BACK of the stained area under cold running water with a white towel underneath. 60–90 seconds. Cold only — heat sets the protein into denim permanently.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean Versatile / Biz / Tide Plus per label, COOL water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dissolve enzyme pretreat in COOL water. Apply to spot, keep damp under a cloth, 15–20 min. Hot water above bath temperature damages enzymes AND sets the protein. Cool only.

spottingProtocol.technique: Indigo bleed test on dark / dyed denim
spottingProtocol.instruction: Inside seam: dab 3% peroxide, blot with white cloth. If indigo transfers, skip peroxide. Light-wash denim is usually fine; raw / dark / selvage often isn't.
Hidden-test-passed denim only: 3% peroxide damp cloth 5 min for residual iron-brown shadow. Damp-cloth rinse. Stop if indigo starts to lift.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Washing machine, cool water, drying rack
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY only. NEVER hot dryer until cleared — locks any residual protein and brown shadow into denim.

steps: 2. Enzyme pretreat in COOL water, 15–20 min — never hot.
3. Hidden-seam peroxide test on dark / dyed denim.
4. 3% H₂O₂ for iron-brown shadow on hidden-test-passed denim, 5 min.
5. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. No heat until cleared.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile / Biz / Tide Plus / liquid Protein Spotter)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets blood protein into denim
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets the protein permanently into denim
NEVER hot dryer until stain fully cleared
NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly, leaves halo
NEVER alcohol on blood — alcohol sets the protein stain
Hidden-seam peroxide test on dark / raw / selvage denim
Always blot, never rub — denim halos under aggressive scrubbing

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Is the Whole First Move
homeSolutions.instruction: Blood on denim is fixable IF you keep it cold. The recurring trap is hot water — it locks the protein into the fabric and turns the iron from red to brown permanently. Two hard rules: NO hot water (sets the stain) and NO chlorine bleach (strips indigo unevenly on denim). Gloves on if it's not your blood — biohazard.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Back-Flush from the BACK
homeSolutions.instruction: Turn the jeans inside out. Hold the BACK of the stained area under cold running water with a white towel underneath the front. Let cold water push the blood out the way it came in. 60 to 90 seconds. Cold only — even warm tap water sets the protein.

Dissolve an enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, or Tide Plus) in COOL water per the label. Apply to the spot, keep damp under a cloth, wait 15 to 20 minutes. The enzyme dismantles the protein at cool temperature. Don't be tempted to use warm water 'to help' — heat above bath temperature damages the enzymes AND sets the protein.

Before any peroxide on dark or indigo denim: dab a drop of 3% peroxide on an inside seam, blot with a white cloth. If indigo transfers to the cloth, skip peroxide — your denim isn't peroxide-safe. Light-wash denim usually passes; raw / dark / selvage often doesn't.

If the hidden-seam test passed: 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth, press the spot 5 minutes, blot. Handles the iron-brown shadow that sometimes remains after the protein is gone. Stop if indigo starts to lift mid-application — the test passed but extended dwell can still strip color.
Cool machine wash with normal detergent. AIR-DRY only. NEVER hot dryer until cleared. Heat at this step locks any residual protein or brown shadow into the denim permanently.

steps: 2. NO hot water. NO chlorine bleach. NO alcohol on blood.
3. Cold water back-flush 60–90 sec from the BACK.
4. Enzyme pretreat in COOL water, 15–20 min.
5. Hidden-seam peroxide test on dark / raw denim.
6. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5 min for iron-brown shadow on hidden-test-passed denim.
7. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. No dryer until cleared.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, Tide Plus)
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot water or hot dryer was already used (often permanent brown shadow). Stop if hidden-seam test shows indigo loss — pro is the right call. Stop on raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim from minute one (pro). Stop if indigo lifts during peroxide application even after test passed.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets blood permanently
NEVER chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly
NEVER alcohol on blood — alcohol sets the protein
Hidden-seam peroxide test on dark / raw denim
Always blot, never rub — denim halos

neverDo: Never hot water on blood + denim
Never rub denim during cleaning — causes halo ring
Never bare-handed if blood isn't your own
fiberSensitivities: Indigo / dark / dyed denim: hidden-seam peroxide test mandatory
Raw / selvage denim: pro from minute one — moisture causes uneven fading
Light-wash denim: most forgiving — full home protocol applicable
Stretch denim with elastane: cool only — peroxide can stress elastane
Black overdyed denim: pro — color-loss risk on any oxidizer

materialWarnings: No hot water — sets blood permanently
No chlorine bleach on denim — strips indigo unevenly
Hidden-seam peroxide test before bounded application on dark denim
Raw / selvage denim = pro from minute one
Always blot, never rub — denim halos

escalation.when: Hot water or hot dryer already used (often permanent brown shadow). Hidden-seam test shows indigo loss. Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim. 2 home rounds plateau. Aged set blood through multiple wash cycles.
escalation.whatToTell: "Blood on [light-wash / dark / indigo / raw / selvage] denim, age [fresh / hours / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [cold back-flush + cool enzyme pretreat + 3% peroxide on hidden-test-passed denim + cool wash + air-dry]. No hot, no chlorine, no alcohol. [Residual brown / indigo loss / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with denim experience plus protein/iron-stain spotting capability

contextQuestions: Light-wash, dark indigo, raw / selvage, or black-overdyed denim?
Fresh, hours old, or hot-washed / hot-dried already?
Has alcohol or chlorine bleach been used on the spot?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Indigo dye loss is the recurring denim risk on blood — hidden-seam peroxide test is non-negotiable. Raw / selvage / black-overdyed denim goes to pro because the dye is more fragile and any oxidizer + scrubbing risks visible damage. Hot-dried blood leaves a permanent brown shadow most of the time; manage expectations. Alcohol pre-applied (rubbing alcohol or hand sanitizer to clean the cut first) often sets the protein — disclose to the cleaner.
